title: Combining Electro-, Photo-, and Biocatalysis for One-Pot Selective Conversion of Furfural into Value-Added C4 Chemicals

gt_route.evidence_quote: the complete conversion of 10 mM furfural was implemented within 4 h when ACT was coupled with EY.

gt_route.evidence_quote: the FCA photo-oxygenation may be the rate-limiting step in the cascade reaction
gt_route.step_notes: Rate-limiting step; enhanced by immobilizing EY on resin (EY@NKA).

gt_route.evidence_quote: ACT is a versatile catalyst capable of oxidizing both furfural and intermediate 5-hydroxy-2-(5H)-furanone.

title: Rhodium-Catalyzed Asymmetric Hydrohydrazonemethylation of Styrenes: Access to Chiral Hydrazones, Hydrazides, Hydrazines and Amines

gt_route.evidence_quote: The anticipated hydroformylation-hydrazone reaction sequence (HHM) occurred and the selective formation of 3 was observed... This product was isolated with a yield of 84% after simple aqueous work-up.
gt_route.step_notes: Hydroformylation and condensation occur in one pot; product precipitates but is used directly.

gt_route.evidence_quote: The reduction was conveniently carried out as a one-pot procedure, directly using the crude reaction mixture from the hydrohydrazonemethylation.
gt_route.step_notes: One-pot reduction using DMAB/TsOH after HHM.

gt_route.evidence_quote: the acid catalyzed methanolysis with hydrogen chloride in MeOH/Et2O under water free conditions led to the selective formation of the monohydrochloride of (R)-(2-phenylpropyl)hydrazine

catalyst_combination_summary: Rh/(R,R)-Ph-BPE for hydroformylation, then DMAB/TsOH for reduction or Raney-Ni for hydrogenolysis

title: Novel Multienzymatic Cascade for the Conversion of Salicin From Plant Extract Into Salicylic Acid

title: One-Pot Biocatalytic Transformation of Adipic Acid to 6-Aminocaproic Acid and 1,6-Hexamethylenediamine Using Carboxylic Acid Reductases and Transaminases

catalyst_components.cofactor_regeneration_mode_raw: enzymatic (PPK2 for ATP, FDH for NADPH)

gt_route.evidence_quote: With 6-ACA as substrate, purified L342E and SAV2585 showed up to 50% conversion to HMD compared to 1.5% observed for wild type MAB4714.
gt_route.step_notes: L342E mutant shows 10-fold higher catalytic efficiency toward 6-ACA than wild type.

gt_route.evidence_quote: Furthermore, the addition of PatA increased the conversion of 6-ACA to HMD to 70%.
gt_route.step_notes: PatA addition increased conversion from 50% to 75%.

global_conditions.additives_text: 2 mM NADPH, 2 mM L-Glu, 2 mM ATP, 5 mM polyP, 10 mM MgCl2, 0.05 mM PLP, 100 mM Na-formate, 50 mM NH4Cl, 20 μg CHU0107, 20 μg FDH D222Q/H224N, 20 μg PPA, 20 μg Glu-DH, 80 μg CAR L342E, 20 μg SAV2585, 20 μg PatA

catalyst_combination_summary: CAR MAB4714 L342E + ωTA SAV2585 + PatA

criteria: all productive steps have rxn_smiles_status == ok

title: Tuning of the enzyme ratio in a neutral redox convergent cascade: A key approach for an efficient one-pot/two-step biocatalytic whole-cell system

gt_route.evidence_quote: The efficiency of a versatile in vivo cascade involving a promiscuous alcohol dehydrogenase, obtained from a biodiversity search, and a Baeyer-Villiger monooxygenase was enhanced by the independent control of the production level of each enzyme to produce ε-caprolactone and 3,4-dihydrocoumarin.

The efficiency of a versatile in vivo cascade involving a promiscuous alcohol dehydrogenase, obtained from a biodiversity search, and a Baeyer-Villiger monooxygenase was enhanced by the independent control of the production level of each enzyme to produce ε-caprolactone and 3,4-dihydrocoumarin.

catalyst_combination_summary: ADH_Singulis (NADP+-dependent) + BVMO_Parvi (NADPH-dependent) in E. coli whole cells

title: One-Pot Biocatalytic Transformation of Adipic Acid to 6-Aminocaproic Acid and 1,6-Hexamethylenediamine Using Carboxylic Acid Reductases and Transaminases

catalyst_components.cofactor_regeneration_mode_raw: enzymatic (PPK2 for ATP, FDH for NADPH)

gt_route.evidence_quote: With regenerating systems for ATP, NAD(P)H, and amine donor, these enzymes achieved 40% conversion, whereas the addition of the E. coli pyrophosphatase PPA increased 6-ACA production to 90-95% conversion.
gt_route.step_notes: CAR reduces adipic acid to adipic semialdehyde, which spontaneously cyclizes? Not specified.

gt_route.evidence_quote: With regenerating systems for ATP, NAD(P)H, and amine donor, these enzymes achieved 40% conversion, whereas the addition of the E. coli pyrophosphatase PPA increased 6-ACA production to 90-95% conversion.
gt_route.step_notes: Transaminase uses L-Glu as amine donor, regenerated by Glu-DH.

global_conditions.additives_text: 2 mM NADPH, 2 mM L-Glu, 2 mM ATP, 5 mM polyP, 10 mM MgCl2, 0.05 mM PLP, 100 mM Na-formate, 50 mM NH4Cl, 20 μg CHU0107 (PPK2), 20 μg FDH D222Q/H224N, 20 μg PPA, 20 μg Glu-DH, 80 μg CAR, 20 μg SAV2585
